hook: So you're telling me I can get paid to learn a trade AND graduate with zero debt?
concept: how apprenticeships pay you to learn
script: Yeah. That's literally how apprenticeships work. [PAUSE] You sign up, you get paired with a master tradesperson, and they PAY YOU to work while you learn. [PAUSE] We're talking $15 to $25 an hour starting out—sometimes more depending on your field. [PAUSE] Meanwhile, your college friend is dropping $40K a year on classes they're gonna forget by summer. [PAUSE] You? You're building actual skills. Real experience. A paycheck. [PAUSE] And here's the kicker—most apprenticeships last 3 to 5 years. By the time you're done, you're not just certified... you're potentially making six figures in skilled trades like electrician, plumbing, or HVAC. [PAUSE] Your college buddy is still paying off student loans. [PAUSE] Check the link in bio for more real info on how to get started with apprenticeships.
